headline: Is Beyond Meat’s (BYND) Functional Beverage Push Quietly Redefining Its Core Brand Strategy?
summary: In April 2026, Beyond Meat, Inc. announced a new distribution deal with Big Geyser to bring its Beyond Immerse functional beverages into more than 26,000 New York–area outlets, while also rolling out its Clean Label Project–certified Beyond Breakfast Sausage lineup nationwide at major retailers such as Kroger and Sprouts Farmers Market. These moves highlight Beyond Meat’s push to broaden its plant-based portfolio beyond meat alternatives, combining beverage expansion with health-focused,...

headline: 1 S&P 500 Stock with Exciting Potential and 2 We Brush Off
summary: While the S&P 500 (^GSPC) includes industry leaders, not every stock in the index is a winner. Some companies are past their prime, weighed down by poor execution, weak financials, or structural headwinds.

headline: MAMA Says a Fresh High Could Come Before Mid-Year
summary: Mama's Creations is a high-quality consumer staple growth story with widening margins and ample cash flow, on track to rally higher in 2026.

headline: Grocers turn to AI pricing as inflation reshapes food shopping
summary: CNBC Food and Wellness Reporter Brandon Gomez reports grocers are using AI to cut food waste and target discounts, boosting sales and traffic as inflation drives consumers to seek deals.

headline: Some grocers are using AI to cut food waste and boost profit margins
summary: Grocers turn to AI pricing to cut waste, protect margins and compete for deal-seeking shoppers—targeting dynamic pricing discounts.

raw.headline: Some grocers are using AI to cut food waste and boost profit margins

headline: How AI is giving Kroger an edge in the grocery price wars and helping to curb food waste
summary: Grocery stores like Kroger are using AI to help find value for perishable food that would otherwise end up in the trash. The data being generated by Flashfood and companies like it are giving retailers insights into consumer behaviors like showing what products will sell, at what price and at what point in their shelf life. CNBC’s Brandon Gomez looks into this growing technology.
